subject: Chemistry
title: Chemiluminescence: Glow Sticks
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Chemiluminescence: Glow Sticks.

hook.text: Snap a glow stick and it glows for hours. No battery, no bulb—just chemistry.

buildup.text: Inside are two chemicals separated by a glass vial. Bending the stick breaks the vial and mixes them.

discovery.text: Hydrogen peroxide reacts with a phenyl oxalate ester, creating an excited intermediate that transfers energy to a dye.

twist.text: Put a glow stick in hot water and it glows brighter but dies faster. Cold slows the reaction and extends it.

climax.text: Military, divers, and concert-goers all rely on this portable chemical light source.

punchline.text: Snap, mix, glow. Light from a chemical reaction. No electricity needed.

quiz.question: Why does a glow stick glow brighter in hot water?
options: Heat speeds up the chemical reaction
Hot water adds energy to the light
